title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Aurora, Illinois
twitter: 🚛 Semi crash in Aurora, IL? The truck's black box data can VANISH in days — carriers aren't required to keep it forever. Act NOW to preserve critical evidence. Call 855 WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or visit https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-aurora-illinois
linkedin:
If you or someone you know was involved in a semi-truck crash in Aurora, Illinois, there is one piece of evidence that could make or break the entire case — and it has an expiration date.

Commercial trucks are equipped with Electronic Control Modules (ECM) and Event Data Recorders (EDR), commonly called black boxes. These devices capture speed, braking patterns, engine performance, and driver behavior in the moments before a collision. In trucking litigation, this data is often the difference between a strong claim and an uphill battle.

Here is the critical issue: trucking carriers are not legally obligated to preserve this data indefinitely. Without a timely spoliation letter or legal hold, that evidence can be overwritten or destroyed — sometimes within days of the crash.

For attorneys in our network of 800+ law firms nationwide, WreckMatch connects you with pre-screened truck accident cases where preservation windows are top of mind. For injury victims in Aurora and across Illinois, understanding your 2-year statute of limitations is just the starting point — acting within the first 48 to 72 hours is where cases are won.

Don't let critical evidence disappear. Connect with a qualified truck accident attorney today.

👉 https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form
facebook:
If a semi-truck hit you in Aurora, Illinois, the truck's black box could be the most powerful piece of evidence in your case — but here's the hard truth: that data may only exist for a matter of days before it's gone forever.

Trucking companies are not always required to preserve black box data after a crash. Without quick legal action, speed records, braking data, and driver behavior logs can be overwritten or legally destroyed. Illinois gives victims a 2-year window to file a claim, but waiting even a few weeks to act could mean losing the evidence that proves what really happened.

If you or a loved one was hit by a commercial truck in the Aurora area, please don't wait to get legal guidance. The sooner an attorney sends a preservation letter, the better your chances of holding the right people accountable.

reddit_title: Hit by a semi in Illinois — does the truck's black box data expire? What should I do first?
reddit_body:
If you were recently hit by a semi-truck in Illinois, one of the most important things to understand is that commercial trucks carry black boxes — called ECMs or EDRs — that record speed, braking, throttle input, and other driver behavior in the seconds before a crash. This data can be incredibly valuable in proving fault, but it does not last forever. Trucking carriers are often not required to preserve it indefinitely, and without a formal legal hold or spoliation letter, it can be overwritten or destroyed within days.

The single most time-sensitive step you can take is contacting a truck accident attorney as quickly as possible — ideally within the first 48 to 72 hours. An attorney can send an immediate preservation demand to the carrier, which creates a legal obligation for them to retain that data. Illinois has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but that deadline is almost irrelevant if key evidence has already been destroyed in the first week.

For anyone navigating this in the Aurora or greater Illinois area, resources like wreckmatch.com can help connect you with attorneys who specifically handle commercial truck cases and understand the urgency around evidence preservation. Don't treat this like a standard fender-bender — semi cases involve federal trucking regulations, carrier liability, and time-sensitive evidence that requires specialized legal experience.
